title: orchis lactea subsp. corsica
intro: Orchis lactea subsp. corsica is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and unique growth habits. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Orchis lactea subsp. corsica are attributed to its ability to improve cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and possess antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
